commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=b8b386e09c41f4879074c97ae5705b7073ab6104)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an opt-in `channels.mattermost.dmReplyToMode` setting and updates Mattermost DM reply/session handling, docs, and unit tests so direct messages can use thread-scoped sessions.

PR surface: Source +23, Tests +55, Docs +7. Total +85 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection on current main shows direct Mattermost chats still force `replyToMode` off and suppress direct thread roots/history; I did not run a live Mattermost workspace in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed because this adds a new Mattermost config/product contract and exact branch real behavior proof; automation cannot settle either blocker.

target.title: feat(mattermost): add opt-in DM threading via dmReplyToMode
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Resolves #93203.

Mattermost direct messages are hardcoded non-threaded, so a DM `@mention` can never start its own threaded session:

- `resolveMattermostReplyToMode` returns `"off"` for direct chats before consulting config.
- `resolveMattermostReplyRootId` / `resolveMattermostEffectiveReplyToId` short-circuit to no thread root for direct chats.

So every DM accretes into one ever-growing session — per-session token usage on that one DM grows without bound, and there's no way to isolate each DM topic the way groups/channels (and Slack) can.

## Fix (opt-in, backward compatible)

Add `channels.mattermost.dmReplyToMode` (same enum as `replyToMode`), **default `"off"`** — existing flat-DM deployments are unchanged. When set to `"first"`/`"all"`, a DM `@mention` starts an independent threaded session.

- `resolveMattermostReplyToMode`: direct chats return `config.dmReplyToMode ?? "off"` (was hardcoded `"off"`).
- `resolveMattermostEffectiveReplyToId`: the direct early-return is gated on `replyToMode === "off"` (the value already reflects `dmReplyToMode` for direct chats), so opt-in DMs fall through to the same thread-root logic as groups/channels.
- `resolveMattermostReplyRootId`: the direct early-return is gated on the absence of a thread root — a DM only carries one when DM threading is enabled.
- New DM threads start as **fresh, independent sessions** (no parent-session inheritance) so each topic is isolated; and a threaded DM keeps its own history key while a flat DM root still keeps none.

## Scope & the "product decision"

The issue carried a `needs-product-decision` label, but the only open question is the **shape of the knob** — `dmReplyToMode` (this PR, mirrors the existing `replyToMode` enum) vs. a boolean `dmThreading`. This PR keeps the historical flat-DM contract intact by **defaulting to `"off"`**, so it changes no existing behavior; happy to rename to a boolean if maintainers prefer.

## What changed

- `extensions/mattermost/src/types.ts` — add `dmReplyToMode?: MattermostReplyToMode` (+ doc).
- `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/accounts.ts` — direct chats honor `dmReplyToMode`.
- `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.ts` — gate the direct-chat reply-root / effective-reply / parent-session / history-key guards on DM threading.
- `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/{accounts,monitor}.test.ts` — opt-in DM threading + preserved flat-DM coverage.
- `docs/channels/mattermost.md` — document `dmReplyToMode`.

## Evidence

- **Unit tests (added):** `dmReplyToMode` unset → DMs stay flat (`off`); set → `resolveMattermostReplyToMode` returns it, DMs get a thread root (existing root reused, or a new thread started under the post), and the DM thread session is fresh (no parent inheritance). Channel/group behavior unchanged.
- **Live Mattermost (deployed build, redacted):** running the equivalent (always-on) DM-threading change in production yields **368 independent DM thread sessions** keyed `…:mattermost:direct:<userId>:thread:<root>`, each with no parent session — i.e. DM `@mention`s reliably create independent, thread-scoped sessions and per-session token usage stays bounded, while flat DMs (the default) remain `…:mattermost:direct:<userId>` with no thread root.
